text: Horrible, disappointing bread. Warburtons snaps when toasted and disintegrates when eating, their “medium sliced” is thinner than any medium sliced bread I’ve ever had. On Slimming World I need brown bread that actually holds together and can make a proper sandwich instead of two pathetic slices. Sainsbury’s claim their own 400g loaf might go to waste, yet Warburtons, the same size and more expensive, clearly sells fine. In my local store, and occasionally when I go to the larger store, there’s always plenty on the shelves. It makes no sense, why not stock your own bread instead? Letting customers down and forcing them to shop elsewhere doesn’t seem to matter to Sainsbury’s.

text: I tried this for the first time, based on reviews, but would never buy it again. It contains palm oil and emulsifiers, bad for health (according to experts). There is no such thing as sustainable palm oil, the growing of which has resulted in huge deforestation, environmental degradation and bad for animals such as orangutans, with ever reducing habitat. title: Oh dear Sainsbury's - your bread is awful
text: So Sainsbury's, you fire all your trained bakers to save money, using normal staff to bake the bread and then expect your bread to stay the same when brought in from outside the store? Well let me help you out here, bringing unbaked dough in that has apparently been proved then frozen does not produce very nice bread at all. The crust is now well, very hard and if you are lucky, it stays on the loaf or as in my photo here of the 400g version shows, it just crumbles off. Your bread used to be the nicest out of the supermarkets, now its worse even than Asda and Tesco. Buy this if you have to folks but don't go out of your way - Morrisons and Asda extra special baked in store bread is so much better.

product_name: Sainsbury's Free From White Sliced Bread
title: Terrible new recipe.
text: 1 star because you can’t give 0. Having been diagnosed with coeliac disease 4 years ago I have no choice but to eat gluten free bread. Sainsbury’s previous white sliced loaf was a life saver with a good texture, shape and appearance of ‘real’ bread. Good flavour and easy to make a sandwich. Sadly everything about this new recipe is a disaster. Weird texture, weird shape and weird flavour. I won’t be buying again.

product_name: Sainsbury's Scotch Egg, Taste the Difference 130g
title: Virtually Tasteless Sausage Meat & Over-Boiled Egg
text: The title says it all, really. How this can be considered a "Taste The Difference" range is beyond me. The meat is improperly seasoned, the egg rubber, dry and hard, the breadcrumb coating not at all crispy, rather it's actually quite sloppy ... and it tastes of virtually nothing. Of late, it's almost as if Sainsbury's add this moniker to anything and everything and subsequently increase the selling price. Wine, ham, scotch eggs ... anything at all, really! Anyways, if you want to taste any difference, do what I will now continue to do: buy from a local butcher or deli instead, where the product is made either the same day or the day before. Just a thought...

Taste the difference! the only difference being how much it cost for such terrible food. Stick to the real thing in future

product_name: Sainsbury's 1kg British Fresh Skinless & Boneless Chicken Breast Fillets
title: Most of the time spoiled or rubbery
text: 9 times out of 10, this is spoiled 3 days before the use-by date. The recent purchase — use-by date is 1 April; today is 29 March. Already smells terrible and is sticky. Stored properly in a 3ºC fridge (it has a thermometer). If you’re lucky and get one with a longer use-by date and manage to use it before it starts smelling, it’s hit-and-miss. Sometimes nice and juicy, but often hard and rubbery (the condition known as woody breast).

product_name: Sainsbury's 640g British Fresh Skinless & Boneless Chicken Breast Fillets
title: Woody texture, avoid
text: Absolutely shocking. We buy a lot of chicken breast weekly and the last few years the quality has been declining. We used to get 4 breasts in this pack, now only 2 unnaturally HUGE ones. The texture of the breasts are hard and the pack comes with loads of gristle and even bone. The chicken breast is ‘woody’ which means the chicken has grown too fast, plus its pumped full of water, resulting in a disgusting crunchy texture no matter how you cook it. We have had some ok breast, but the bad ones are happening way too often now. Oh and don’t forget the price rises too! I never write reviews but i’m having to throw away way too much chicken recently!!

title: Awful.
text: I have been buying these kyivs for a long time and they have been consistently excellent. Unfortunately they have changed and not in a good way, Sainsbury must now be buying older birds as the chicken is stringy, tough and inedible. They are empty of the garlic butter essential for kyivs, and the so called “panko” crumb they are coated in burns black round the edges when cooked at the recommended temperature and time, resulting in a product that looks unsightly and unattractive. product_name: Sainsbury's 2kg British Fresh Skinless & Boneless Chicken Breast Fillets
title: Now utterly disgusting
text: I've been buying these for years, but never again. I've noticed they're getting more and more watery. The last pack I bought, the breasts were enormous - I used to keep chickens and I've never seen a chicken that could produce a breast that large. Really weird and unpleasant texture - I thought I'd undercooked it, so cooked a bit longer - it was rubbery, in places almost crunchy and hard. Also noticed how poor the butchery is with the breasts and also the thighs - loads of bits of bone, often rib bones, which shouldn't be there. All adds to the weight I suppose, another rip off. When roasting them, they're not actually roasted, they're boiled in their own water and shrink to nothing. A lot of Sainsbury's produce starts to turn before its use by date - you need to sort out your suppliers - very disappointing, I'm not only a customer but a shareholder thinking of putting my money elsewhere

product_name: Sainsbury's Cajun British Chicken Breast Grills 330g
title: Inedible Cajun Chicken
text: I have no idea where Sainsbury's source this from but the Chicken is disgusting. Maybe I got a bad batch but the quality is below 1 star. I thoroughly cooked half of the chicken 3 days before it's use by date and I could already tell that the quality of the chicken was substandard by the amount of water and only what I can describe as chicken surface debris that was coming off it ... and it wasn't the Cajun coating. I tried eating it but it was actually chewable in a very unpleasant way. Literally two bites and the rest went in the bin as will the other uncooked half. Some people have reviewed this 5 star and others have found it like me way below what you would expect from Sainsbury's. Clearly quality control is amiss here for there to be such wide disparaging views. I won't add photos as I don't want to put you off chicken for life just be aware that the quality of this product is very variable. Very disappointed ... I don't normally review things unless I have a very strong positive or negative experience.

title: Sizes have changed
text: What is going on now with these. I have been buying them for years and there was always two equal size chicken breasts but for the last month or so it has changed to one regular sized chicken breast and one very small one. I thought this was an accident the first time but no it has been over and over again. I can’t believe you are doing this probably in the name of shrinkflation but please stop it. I’m starting to shop more and more elsewhere due to your poor quality smaller items that have started to appear in my delivered shop.

product_name: Sainsbury's Slow Cooked Chicken Breasts 470g
title: Horrible flavour and strange textured chicken
text: Have always been disappointed with Sainsbury’s ready meals but I was in a hurry and fell for the photo on the packaging. Never again – I have learnt my lesson. Expensive for what you got. Revolting unnatural chemical taste. Sauce not creamy at all - it was grey and watery – very few mushrooms or bacon and couldn’t see or taste the spinach at all. Hard to decide which was the more unpleasant flavour between the overpowering smokiness or the citrus/vinegar background flavour. Would like to say the chicken was tender but it was just a strange ‘soft’ texture. Back to the drawing board I think.

product_name: Sainsbury's 2kg British Fresh Chicken Skin on Thighs
title: Yuk!!!!
text: This has to be the worst, fattiest, chewiest, most disgusting chicken I've ever tasted, if anyone feeds this to an animal they should be publicly hanged for extreme animal cruelty. Ok, maybe I just got unlucky compared to other reviewers, but honestly Sainsbury's, your farmers did you a really bad turn when they put this dreck into your supply train. I've always bought beef & lamb products from Sainsbury's on the grounds it's generally excellent, however this is the absolutely last time I'll ever buy chicken from them, worst part is, thanks to the online form only accepting one submission for refund per order I'm now out of pocket to the tune of one 2kg pack of chicken thighs & one 2kg pack of chicken drumsticks (these were equally disgusting) which means you have one seriously unhappy customer on your hands, especially as I had to chuck what proved to be inedible rubbish straight into my food recycling bin.

product_name: Ko-Lee Instant Noodles Chicken Flavour 70g
title: They’re far too thin, resulting in a limp, unsatis
text: I recently tried these instant noodles hoping for a quick, flavorful meal, but I was left disappointed. The noodles lack authenticity and fail to capture the rich, savory Chinese taste typically associated with instant noodles. They’re far too thin, resulting in a limp, unsatisfying texture that doesn’t hold up in the broth. The seasoning misses the mark, lacking the bold, umami depth I expect from Chinese-inspired noodles. Overall, these noodles fall short of delivering a genuine or enjoyable experience. I won’t be reaching for them again.

product_name: Sainsbury's British or Irish Extra Lean Diced Beef 500g
title: Feeling ripped off
text: I don't mind the new packaging but selling this as 500g of EXTRA LEAN diced beef is misleading. Firstly, it was 8% short on weight and that's more than can be explained by the change in packaging. Secondly, it is most definitely not EXTRA LEAN ! We had to trim off so much fat that we were only left with enough edible meat for a very small portion each so had to supplement the meal by cooking some yorkshire puddings. Would have put this down to a one-off but clearly it's not as a friend had a similar experience with buying this item.

I can only imagine the people giving this product good reviews are not foodies, or have forgotten what mince should be like

title: Terrible packaging
text: I’m sure there is a massive cost saving for Sainsbury’s with the new packaging, there may even be an environmental benefit too but it’s terrible for the mince. Because it’s compressed into the new packet, the mince comes out in a solid block and it is really difficult to break the mince up into nice small pieces. Shame because the mince seemed to be of reasonable quality, it’s just not good to cook with. Hopefully, not all supermarkets will go down this route, so I’ll still be able to get decent minced beef.

title: Quality has become unacceptable
text: I have purchased this joint for sunday lunch regularly over several years. The quality has been consistently good but it is no longer. Three weeks ago, the joint contained a large amount of gristle and the meat was tough and tasteless. I thought this must be an unfortunate one off. The following week it was just the same. Last week was the last straw and a third of the joint has to be discarded. This takes the matter beyond coincidence and it appears that Sainsbury's has adopted a new inferior and unacceptable standard for this product despite the 50% increase in price. I am now purchasing the sunday joint elsewhere.

product_name: Sainsbury's British or Irish Diced Beef 500g
title: Not good
text: If you’re buying this to be used as dog food then your dog will probably be fine with it.. generally I don’t think dogs are too bothered about a particular cut of beef, like this one. Shin in this case. As for humans, I don’t really think many guests would appreciate the very poor quality. I opted for this cut over a leaner cut because I wanted more vitamin A but I didn’t expect it to be beef shin. I’ve never had beef shin before but it’s full of gross bits that have no place in a casserole, they might as well be throwing the penis in as well. I just want the butt, I’ll go for silverside next time. It has much better reviews. This should be considered for dog food only. The extra lean I have tried before in burritos and that was okay, but not brilliant. I think it was probably an acceptable cut though and not beef shin.

product_name: Sainsbury's 30 Days Matured British Beef Picanha Steak, Taste the Difference 225g
title: Dire. Sinewy and inedible
text: I have only tried this cut in a restaurant and really enjoyed it. Bought it on Monday, eating (well, trying to) on Wednesday. Utterly unacceptable quality, even at half the (full) price paid. It looks (on both sides) to be a lean steak. But it is just flesh irrevocably melded onto inedible sinew. Chew for a minute and you’ll still not have made any in-roads. I’ve ended up with a half-untouched steak and several discarded, chewed lumps on the side of my plate. I rarely get annoyed enough to return items but there is going to be a rumpus if I don’t get my money back. I think (haven’t got my receipt to hand) that a single steak was in the region of £7 for one. Frankly, 50 pence would be fifty pence too much. Utterly vile and disappointing.

The fact that the 'jus' came in a plastic bag was more than irritating - not exactly environmentally friendly, not recyclable. Would not recommend to anyone and would not feed it to the dog. It went into the food recycling bin.

The second en croute in the same pack when cooked was somewhat better, a more generous piece of salmon and reasonable amount of sauce BUT in both portions in the package, just one prawn per serving, really pathetic!! title: Contains meat.Avoid if dog has meat allergies.

product_name: Sainsbury's Apple & Mango High Juice Squash 1L
title: Recipe changed - Now it’s awful!
text: I have been making my feelings known on Twitter, the change from using Alphonso Mango to just Mango is a disaster. Alphonso Mango has a naturally sweeter and stronger taste, this new choice, (which I have been told is being sold alongside the original, I believe this to be untrue as I have yet to see evidence of this), the new choice is revolting, it is weak, meaning more squash is required, this means more product is used. The only saving grace is that Sainsbury’s finally reduced the price, but that doesn’t take away from this now awful product. As others have already mentioned, this was the only reason I shopped in Sainsbury’s. I would much rather pay more and have the original product back on the shelves. In addition to that, I contacted Customer Service twice and was assured I would be emailed with a reply to their investigations, twice I heard nothing and I therefore took to Twitter, I urge others to do the same. Sort it out Sainsbury’s, the product is dire, the Customer Service is just as bad. Not good enough!

product_name: Kellogg's All-Bran Breakfast Cereal 750g
title: Appalling new recipe All Bran
text: I have been eating Kellogg's All Bran Original for breakfast for 67 years and was appalled to discover that Kellogg's had changed the recipe without any forewarning. Like everyone else here, I can't stand the new Fibre Plus version as it tastes and looks nothing like the original. To make matters worse there's less fibre in the new recipe. As with other reviewers, I kept receiving Fibre Plus from Sainsbury's, even though I'd ordered All Bran Original, and returned the box unopened every time. After several weeks of this happening, and unaware at that time of the recipe change, I emailed Sainsbury's to advise them that there appeared to be an issue with their website, which was, and still is, showing the product as All Bran Original. They told me they would investigate and correct the error. Obviously, that hasn't happened. I checked the other major supermarket sites and discovered that their customers were also returning boxes of the Fibre Plus because they didn't like the recipe. I also contacted Kellogg's directly, through their website, and asked why I could no longer obtain All Bran Original. They replied that, after extensive research, and conducting focus groups, they felt that the new recipe was a big improvement on the original because it no longer produced a pile of dust in the bottom of the wrapping, and it was cheaper to produce. I'm hoping that, with so many of us returning the Fibre Plus, Kellogg's will get the message that they've made a huge mistake and reinstate the Original recipe. I would advise other devotees to contact Kellogg's direct.

product_name: Kellogg's All-Bran Breakfast Cereal 750g
title: New recipe totally ruined this once great cereal
text: I'm fairly new to All-bran but loved it's malty taste and it's great texture. Sadly that all changed with the new recipe from Kellogs, brought in to save them costs in production. It's now tasteless, and when others say it tastes like cardboard, it genuinely does. It no longer soaks up the milk - it's fibre, fibre is naturally absorbent yet this new stuff looks like it's got some kind of coating on it to prevent this. My search is now on to find a replacement for All-bran.

title: Terrible new recipe
text: Having enjoyed eating this every day for about 20 years I'm gutted that they've completely ruined it. The two things that distinguished it from poorer alternatives ie the malty flavour and subtle texture that softened with milk are both now gone, and yet they dare to call it improved. Also the new crunchiness that Kelloggs boast of will make it very difficult for many people including myself to eat it, but then it now tastes so bland I'm not sure many people will want to. Well done Kelloggs!

product_name: Alpen Muesli No Added Sugar 1.1kg
title: What is THIS?!
text: Must've been years ago I had true Alpen, and it was good but far too sweet. So finally returning to 'Alpen' I chose this 'no added sugar' version. Alpen is NOT muesli any more - it's 'cheap' (overpriced) ingredients that tastes like Wheatabix and bran - not nice. All it is now is tiny brown wheat or bran flakes, with the texture or flat Rice Crispies, with a handful of cheap porridge oats chucked in, along with a few rock solid raisins, that's it! The ingredients claims 2% sliced nuts, but they don't exist. Really, really bad, and definitely not muesli or anything like the original Alpen, which no longer exists, as the 'Original' version (with sugar) is the same abomination. It used to be so nice, just nuts and oats with lots of high protein whey powder making a thick tasty stodge. Now it's just cheap and nasty, seemingly made from broken Wheatabix. Shameless! Shocking and sad.

title: "30% more 'fruit' " ?!
text: I really wasn’t impressed, unfortunately. I found it too sweet, too dusty, and not even that tasty. They make a big deal of “30% More Fruit” - well, there can’t have been many raisins before because there certainly aren’t many currently. I also think it’s dubious touting “fruit” when the only fruit is dried raisins, probably trying to sound ‘healthy,’ which it really isn’t with the sugar. As for the roasted sliced almonds & hazelnuts, they were hardly noticeable. I’ve had much better own brand mueslis.

product_name: Ambrosia Ready to Eat Porridge Oat Cereal Pot Golden Syrup 210g
title: Truly awful
text: I can't believe how positive the reviews are for this, so I can only assume they're fake. Bought this as I thought it would be super convenient. And it is. But it's also the worst porridge I've ever eaten. So I would describe this more as overcooked porridge soup. The ratio of liquid to porridge is wrong. The flavour does not make up for it. You have that typical ambrosia type creamy taste to it, but it's not hugely pleasant with the mushy sparsely placed oats. This is nothing like the own brand or other bigger brands you're more likely used to - and looks nothing like the picture on the front. I think it said about 11% oats - what ever the percentage it just doesn't seem enough, or they've been so overcooked they've become part of the liquid. I followed all microwave directions to the letter. Such a shame. Give it a go though, maybe it's just me?!

product_name: Jammie Dodgers 140g Raspberry Flavour Biscuits
title: They’ve ruined them with the jam change!
text: I used to really like these and bought them regularly. Recently they’ve changed the jam - new packaging which says “new fruitier jam”. The jam doesn’t taste much different but it’s tough and chewy, and even more annoyingly, it’s now only in the middle of the biscuit, it doesn’t go to the edges, so there is quite a big gap between the two biscuit layers, which then means when you bite into it the biscuit crumbles apart! I wonder if the “new fruitier jam” is just an excuse to use less jam and make it cheaper to produce! If so id rather they had kept them how they were and just put the price up instead!

product_name: Sainsbury's Butter Puffs Biscuits 200g
title: False Advertising
text: These crackers are labelled as being Butter Puffs, however when I received the last pack it turns out that they are no longer made with butter as an ingredient at all. Instead they are now made with palm oil. The end result is a cloying, unpleasant tasting cracker. They crumble badly now rather than being nicely flaky which is down to using oil instead of butter - it just does not have the correct properties to be folded in to create the puff pastry. I ate them so as to not have them go to waste, but it was not an enjoyable experience and I will not be buying them again. I will have to look elsewhere for Butter Puffs which actually still use butter!

title: Alta Rica awful new taste
text: Have been drinking Alta Rica for a long time now. Recently when opened a new jar (with the new label for CO2 reduction), immediately noticed the lack of distinctive aroma when broke the seal on top. Then discovered the taste also was very bland and nothing like the previous jar of Alta Rica. Initially thought it might have been a one off jar or something, purchased another and the very same very bland taste from that one also. I realise others have been told all that has changed is the label, but I refuse to agree with that. IMO something else has changed as the aroma and the taste is now completely different and very bland. Will not be purchasing anymore Alta Rica until I hear somewhere that it has reverted back to the taste it was.

product_name: Kenco Cappuccino Unsweetened Instant Coffee 8 Sachets
title: Unfortunately spoiled by too weak coffee flavour
text: A pleasant hot creamy, frothy, milk beverage with relatively low sugar but certainly far too weak coffee flavour. The Nescafe equivalent has, in our opinion, about the right strength of flavour but is about 50% more expensive at full price levels. Why no own brand equivalent anywhere ? - Morrisons used to do one but has not done so since shortly after the recent change of ownership. This Kenco version is a pale imitation of the market leader and should be reformulated. Ok if you add a half teaspoonful of a good 'gold' instant coffee, but that should already be in the contents. In our opinion ruined by the current formulation, which could so easily be changed to rival that same market leader.

product_name: PG Tips Original Tea Bags x240 696g
title: Absolutely revolting
text: I have to buy this as my partner likes it but goodness knows why! I drank it for years but gave up 6 plus years ago as it had no flavour. The other week I ran out of proper tea (leaf tea!) and so I gave PG another go out of desperation for a morning cuppa - it is awful. It is so bad I am HIGHLY SUSPICIOUS of the other good reviews on here. If you open up a tea bag, you won't find any leaves, just some tea dust. No surprise that Lipton (those who have long provided weak tea to Europe) owns PG. We always said Lipton tastes like it is made with what they swept up and this PG is the same. I imagine the original developer of the brand would be utterly horrified.

title: Mediocre quality of tea
text: Having tried different flavours of tea under the pukka brand after a colleague brought in many pukka boxes of tea labeled with their individual flavors (for us to share during tea breaks), I was appalled by the taste of these tea bags despite having infused them in hot water. Pukka teas are so weak and bland as if I could not taste the flavour and aroma in them. It was worse than drinking plain hot water and I had to throw away the rest of the tea after 3 sips and struggling to enjoy the rest of it. After such experience, I would avoid any pukka brand of tea. My partner and colleagues even mentioned that pukka teas are crap. Even PG tips and sainbury’s own brand of teas taste a lot better although the best tasting teas are Miles and Yorkshire brands.

For that reason cooking time has been reduced and you are no longer advised to turn it part way through. Instead of starting to heat the sauce part way through the cooking, you are now advised to put it in the oven at the same time as chicken. Changed cooking instructions to "overcome" poorer main ingredient - the chicken!

text: Bought this before, but this time sauce was weak, flavourless, just colour, and tasting as if water were added to ingredients after cooked. Paneer if it is not Tofu, seems to have been added after, as it is not blended with the rest. The sauce is not even thick. This was a poor flavour, colour but not spice, certainly nowhere near an authentic dish, as you would experience at an Indian eatery. Better to buy a rich sauce, and blend yr own, with copious amounts compared to these overpriced, under spiced ranges.,

title: A step too far
text: Twice my evening meal has needed to be binned with me left having a sandwich. Both occasions due to absolutely ghastly product. Used to like a Cumberland Pie as they were tasty and a lazy supper. There seemed to be little content in the tray (delivered) so I reduced the cooking time (microwave). Even with taking this precaution the end result was dried out tasteless mash with NO cheese on top and at a guess about 1 teaspoonful of tasteless gravy with one incinerated tiny piece of carrot. The mash was stuck together and difficult to get a fork into it. The actual content of the tray did not reach halfway up and these trays are now quite shallow. I bought under the offer two for 6.00. The other purchase was the cod in parsley sauce... that too was dreadful although this Cumberland Pie took the prize for being worst.

product_name: Pizza Express American Pepperoni Pizza 250g
title: Brand Name, Zero Flavor: Even Sainsbury’s Budget P
text: Pizza Express American Pepperoni Pizza 250g gets a 1 out of 5 from me. For something priced around £5, it’s shockingly underwhelming — tiny, bland, and forgettable. The pepperoni is sparse, the cheese doesn’t melt well, and the crust tastes more like cardboard than restaurant quality. Honestly, even the cheap Sainsbury’s frozen pepperoni pizza — which costs less than half as much — tastes far better, with more topping, better flavour, and a crispier base. This Pizza Express one just feels like you’re paying for the brand name, not the quality

title: Bland, Pizza? no cheese inside? just dry and hard
text: Smell 'none' dough balls hard despite following cooking instructions using my phone alarm for the 6 minutes cooking time, the first time I've ever seen pizza dough balls with no mozzarella cheese inside, I don't understand the point of these hard dry dough balls, with a garlic butter that is only enough to coat about 5 dough balls, to give any flavour, and reduce the dryness, to reduce the dry thick layer of flour that they're coated in, that makes you choke, I won't be buying them again, I can make the same much cheaper, and add my own seasonings, if I wanted plain dough, but pizza dough balls should always contain cheese, there's nothing pizza about these, they're plain dough balls, in a pizza express box, but have no place on a pizza, or correlation with eating a pizza.

title: Not easy to use for several reasons

Because of the shape (rectangular not round) it didn't fit on my pizza stone, which is a pity because it may have cooked better. I didn't overload it with toppings, but even so it didn't cook through, remaining doughy in the middle despite it starting to char on the edges. product_name: The Northern Dough Co. Pizza Dough Original 2x220g
title: Nice idea but thawing instructions aren't right
text: I've purchased this 3-4 times and it just doesn't really work out as it's meant to. I've tried all suggested methods of defrost: 4h room temp, overnight in the fridge etc and it's absolutely not enough to get that elasticity required. Example, I've got one in front of me now which has been 24 hours in the fridge and is still nowhere near being ready for stretch. It does taste nice but it's just too much of a faff to shape and press - by hand or rolling pin.

title: Rubbish! Make your own dough with flour and yeast.
text: This product provides two balls of dough that each make a 12" or so pizza base. I followed the instructions with care for the first ball of dough and the pizza base was absolute rubbish. The dough did not rise well and the texture and flavour was in no way worthy of note. I made the second ball of dough into pita bread which was okay but not remarkable.

product_name: Goodfella's New York Style Ultimate Four Cheese Pizza 534g
title: Underwhelming and under cheesy.
text: I don’t tend to buy frozen pizzas, but I was really hopeful for this one, given it’s from Goodfella’s. Unfortunately the name and reputation didn’t follow through with the quality of the pizza. When cooked for 18 minutes at the recommended 180° the pizza was definitely overcooked, yet the cheese topping didn’t have the stringy ooziness that we’re used to; instead the cheese didn’t seem overly melted. Based on this, it didn’t look particularly appealing, but most disappointing was the taste - it was pretty bland and certainly not something that I’ll be rushing out to buy again, unfortunately. We were definitely all left feeling hungry, which is a real pity.

product_name: Chicago Town Takeaway Large Cheesy Stuffed Pepperoni Pizza 640g
title: Just awful !
text: I was really hoping for a satisfying pizza experience, but this was hands down one of the worst pizzas I’ve ever had. From the moment I took the first bite, I was disappointed. The middle of the pizza is soggy and mushy, making it almost impossible to enjoy. Despite leaving it in the oven for longer than usual, it never seemed to cook properly—still looking and tasting undercooked, even after extra time. The "cheese stuffed" crust? Don’t believe the hype. It’s either vastly overstated or just a failed attempt at creating something exciting. What was promised as a cheesy, indulgent experience turned out to be bland, tasteless, and not worth the calories. I felt full after eating, but it was the kind of fullness that leaves you unsatisfied and a little queasy. Even with frequent discounts, I’d advise steering clear of this pizza. It’s a disappointing mess that doesn’t deliver on any front, and no discount is worth the regret that comes with eating it.

product_name: Mackie's of Scotland Luxury Traditional Real Dairy Ice Cream 1L
title: Nowhere near as good as it used to be
text: We've had this many times over the years, and it's been really nice. Unfortunately, the last tubs have been pretty poor. It still tastes reasonable enough, but the texture is terrible. It used to be proper thick stuff, a bit hard to get out of the tub. However, now it's mushy semi-liquid - something like the texture of double cream, not ice cream. We thought our freezer must have failed when we got it out, but no, it's this ice cream. It's sold by volume, not weight. We weighed it - at something like 550g for a litre, it's not much more than half the density of water - it must contain a lot of air. Sadly, we'd bought six tubs based on how it used to be: we'll go elsewhere next time.

The chocolate is okay, if not very creamy. Size is fine, three in a box is a big stingy but whatever. It's perfectly edible and isn't offensive, but for the luxury brand that Magnum has been historically marketed as, this is just a bit cheap and boring.

title: Rock Bears
text: I really don't understand the positive reviews on this. I paid for this product (unlike the free positive reviews) and though the ice-cream itself it nice, there is hardly any of it for the price, not even a "child-size portion". product_name: Aunt Bessie's Carrot & Swede Mash 500g
title: Unpleasant and watery
text: In theory this sounded like a convenient, healthy, and quick option for a vegetable side dish - particularly as swede is not the easiest of vegetables to peel and prepare. While certainly fast and simple to cook, it turned out to be extremely unpleasant in both taste and texture. The overwhelming majority of the product appeared to be carrot, with several rock hard, woody looking lumps remaining even after the recommended cooking time. The watery, slushy texture and complete lack of any flavour make this a product I would neither recommend nor purchase again.

product_name: Sainsbury's So Organic Garden Peas 500g
title: Sadly inconsistent
text: I regularly buy these peas and sometimes they are amazing! But sadly they are sometimes really bad - some good peas in the bag but they have clearly been mixed with a batch of bad (hard, tasteless) peas making the whole lot unpalatable. I had to return 4 bags today as they were just not edible. Once I've found a good bag, I look for the 'best before date' and try to just buy the ones with the same date. The issue is definitely batch related. I love to buy organic as I don't like to eat pesticide residues so I will still buy these but I'm afraid I will have to return the bad bags.

product_name: Campo Viejo Rioja Reserva 75cl
title: Left a sour taste (Literally)
text: I recently poured myself a bottle of Campo Viejo Reserva Rioja and, to be honest, I was disappointed. From the first sip I noticed a sour edge that lingered unpleasantly, and the finish left a bad taste in my mouth rather than the smooth, refined finish I’d been hoping for. The fruity notes that I’d expected seemed suppressed, and instead I was left with acidity that felt sharp and a bit unbalanced for my taste.

title: Review of Jam Shed Shiraz Red Wine
text: Not for dry wine lover. I really didn’t enjoy this wine. It is overwhelmingly sweet, almost like drinking watered-down jam, and any hint of sourness or dryness that follows just makes it more unbalanced. For anyone who actually knows their preferences or appreciates wine, I wouldn’t recommend this at all. It’s far too sweet for a Shiraz, lacking structure and complexity. I won’t be purchasing it again, and I wouldn’t suggest it to others. That is unfortunate but it’s true. GIFTED #HomeTesteClubTester

product_name: Sainsbury's Ginger Wine 70cl
title: Oh no - you've just gone and done it again :-(
text: I stopped buying your Cabernet Sauvignon Wine Boxes when you watered them down from 14%ABV to 11%ABV to reduce the tax, and they tasted watery and stale. Then you interfered with the Merlot Wine Boxes - watery and stale-smelling too. Now you've done it to your Ginger Wine - watered down to 11% to escape the extra duty. Here's a thing: You could have put the price up, and kept the same (formerly) fine product, with the new price paying the higher tax. Or you could have offered a choice - watered down or the same fine product at a higher price. But there's a hidden aspect here that most people might miss... The most important thing is, if you water it down, the same product now fills more bottles, so the price should go DOWN... This is a sneaky shrinkflation and a watery product all at the same time. Shocking. I'll buy Stones in future. They seem devoid of Sainsbury's deception...

product_name: Sainsbury's Pure Orange Juice With Bits 1L
title: Lovely juice but awful cap - go back to screw-top!
text: I completely agree with previous comments - why on earth have Sainsbury's reverted to the old flat, lift-up tab opening instead of the easy-open, well-sealed screw top? It's definitely not for the convenience of the customer! The lift-up top leaks when you shake the carton to distribute the delicious juicy bits, and can be impossible to open (it's difficult at best, as the force needed to push down on the tab can often squash the top of the carton). Twice in the past 3 weeks I've received cartons on my delivery which have so much thick, rigid glue all around the top that it's simply impossible to open, even after hazardous attempts at prising it with a knife, and I've had to resort to cutting the top the make a spout. This isn't cheap juice, so why should I have to do that? Sainsbury's, listen to your customers, please!

product_name: Vanish Gold Oxi Action 450g Laundry Stain Remover Powder Colour
title: It ruined my clothes
text: If I could, I would give it zero stars. I followed the instructions to soak my t-shirts (1 scoop in 4 litres of reasonably hot water), left it for less than 30 min as instructed, and my t-shirts are now full of tiny dots of bleached stains. Apparently, this powder does not dissolve properly even in hot water (maybe too much for the recommended volume?) and the granules in contact with the fabric start to attack and bleach it (no chlorine doesn't mean no bleach!), leaving unsightly discoloured stains all over the garment. If you still decide to buy it, I also advise you to be careful when you add it to your laundry in the washing machine, if it doesn't dissolve, you'll have to say good bye to all your washing. Lesson learned the hard way unfortunately. Never again...

title: Not soft or cushioned!
text: I am so disappointed with this product. It states clearly on the packaging that the toilet paper is 'Super Softest' and 'cushioned' - it is neither of those things. I wish I had read the other reviews before making my purchase as many other customers say the same thing. This is not the luxury toilet paper I was expecting - far from it - and I certainly will not be buying it again. My 1 star rating is because I felt I had been mislead. I am just surprised that a quality retail business such as Saninburys has not made more of an effort to ensure that quality of the product actually matches the description.

title: Shock digit in the batty
text: There is absolutely no hope of pulling a single wipe out of the packet, it comes out like handkerchiefs from a wizard's sleeve (reminds me of my ex). The packaging is so poorly designed that in order to continue pulling the wipes out, it often rips meaning the wipes dry out very quickly and become pointless as they're not 'wet' wipes anymore. Plus, every wipe is very fragile and nobody wants a surprise finger in the starfish as an ambush on a Thursday morning. Good idea Mr Sainsbury, perhaps work on the quality.

product_name: Sainsbury's Butter Croissants, Taste the Difference x4
title: Not Up to Taste the Difference Standards
text: These butter croissants were a real let‑down, especially compared with the usual quality of the Taste the Difference range. Like the last couple of disappointed reviewers, the issues are the same. Some croissants feel flat and doughy, others overly dry, and none have that proper flaky lift or buttery aroma you’d expect at this price point. They look premium, but the eating experience just doesn’t match. From a health perspective, croissants are naturally high in saturated fat and refined carbs, so when the flavour and texture don’t deliver, it makes the indulgence feel even less worthwhile. For a treat item, you want it to feel special — and these simply don’t. A rare miss for the range and not something I’d rush to buy again.

product_name: Sainsbury's Butter Croissants, Taste the Difference x4
title: Decline in quality?
text: These were our go to croissants for years. Really flaky, buttery and better than others at high end supermarkets. Sadly, our latest pack has shown a big decline in quality. We once purchased the cheaper croissants as the 'taste the difference' ones were out of stock and these now seem to be a duplicate of the cheaper ones, just bigger? Unless it's just a bad batch, I can only assume that this is down to a cost cutting measure as is happening everywhere. Seems ironic they are confident customers won't be able to 'taste the difference'!

product_name: Sainsbury's Breaded Chunky MSC Cod Loins, Taste the Difference x2 350g
title: Why garlic?
text: I really wish that I had read the reviews before purchasing this product because I am totally with the other reviewers who are completely turned off by the overpowering garlic taste! I stopped buying the beer battered fish because the taste of beer overpowers the fish and they are very oily. So decided to try breadcrumbed fish thinking that it will be far less oily and no beer involved. But this garlic taste is ridiculous, nobody wants a nice piece of quality cod overpowered with garlic! We want to be able to taste the fish! The nice light, non oily herby batter used to cover the lemon sole works soo much better. It isn't too thick and the flavour doesn't overpower the fish, it compliments it. Please stop massacring lovely pieces of fish!

product_name: Sainsbury's Smoked Air Dried Dry Cured Back Bacon Rashers, Taste the Difference x6 220g
title: Why Are you adding sugar?
text: I am totally baffled why food producers put sugar in everything. I have been mindful of additives and unnecessary sugar in foods for many years, and check all processed food for it. Although I do not eat much bacon, have become aware of the issues with nitrates so have started checking bacon for them only to find there is now added sugar in bacon. I am astounded, I thought I knew all food products that have added sugar. This surreptitious addition of sugar as well as sugar alternatives to everything is changing our taste buds so we no longer recognize what real food tastes like. I am going to check out my local butcher to see what they have. The prices are a challenge for me but I would rather eat less meat than eat food with unnecessary sugar in it and will never again buy bacon from Sainsbury's.

text: Thought I’d splash out on a better quality bacon than usual as we’ve not had a bacon sandwich lately. product_name: Sainsbury's Petits Pois & Ham Soup, Taste the Difference 600g
title: Absolutely not and never again
text: I have tried many a soup and this is by far the worst soup I have had to endure. We actually both just put it down the drain in the end. Im not sure where they get the ham from but there was absolutely not flavour of it. If spending an hour in a bath made entirely from petits pois sounds like an hour worth spending then this is the soup for you. If you would rather have an edible soup then I highly suggest you look elsewhere.

title: All Fat

product_name: Sainsbury's Smoked Air Dried Bacon Lardons, Taste the Difference 160g
title: Pancetta with lard
text: This was a really bad pack! Very disappointing. Half the pieces were pancetta and the other half was pure lard with not one bit of red meat on, I just chucked those pieces in the bin. The quality of the remaining pancetta was still good and good taste, but I only had maybe 3/4 of the amount I had paid for because quite a few pieces had not be binned. Got it when it was on offer. If it was on offer because they knew they had filled it with fatty / pure lard cuts then it was never really an offer. It should have been on clearance instead. Also why is the nutritional data completely incorrect?? If each unit is 160g and consists of two packs then each pack must be 80g, yet the values per pack are not 8/10ths the value of the per 100g. It’s got an error of 65% if you look because if the total fat is 20g per 100g then it should follow that it is 16g per 80g, yet it’s listed as 9.8g?? Which one is even correct? Are either of these values correct?? It’s not just the fat it’s the whole nutritional values for every macro and calories. That was just an example. This is stupid. Do they not have this on a computer database somewhere? Why is it not picking up any errors in the data automatically? It is it an intentional effort to mislead people into thinking it’s healthier than it really is??

title: Vinegary quiche
text: I have bought these regularly for years but the last few have not tasted good, possibly vinegary? Like poor scrambled eggs rather than delicious quiche. I’ve finally trained myself to stop buying it until there is a recipe change. The only taste the difference is the lack of taste from the preferred previous recipe. In reading the other reviews I wonder whether there is a lack of consistency in production and we get a bad batch?
